title: Mode Router Config
description: Configuration for rootnode-mode-router. Defines the rule set the router evaluates to select an active profile. Same shape conventions as other rootnode runtime schemas: schema_version, name, description, plus router-specific fields (default_profile, rules, manual_override).

schema_version.description: Semantic version of the router config schema. Current version: 1.0.0.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this router config does and when it applies.

default_profile.description: Profile name to return when no rule matches. Required — guarantees the router always returns a routing decision.

rules.description: Ordered list of routing rules. Evaluated in priority order (lowest priority number first). The first rule whose trigger matches the context returns its profile.

rule_id.description: Unique identifier for this rule within the config. Used in evaluation traces.

priority.description: Evaluation order. Lower numbers evaluated first. Use sparse numbering (10, 20, 30) to allow inserting new rules without renumbering.

profile.description: Profile name to return when this rule's trigger matches.

description.description: Optional. Human-readable description of what this rule represents.

set_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when override was set.

expires_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when override expires. After this, override is ignored and rules-based routing resumes.

reason.description: Optional. User-supplied note about why the override was set.

manual_override.description: Active manual override, if any. When non-null and not expired, takes precedence over all rules. Null when no override is active.

notes.description: Optional. Free-text notes about this router config.

time_zone.description: IANA timezone name (e.g., 'America/Phoenix'). If omitted, uses the timezone of the caller-supplied current_time.

end_time.description: HH:MM in 24-hour format. May be earlier than start_time to indicate a window crossing midnight (e.g., 22:00 → 06:00).

category.description: Required for 'event_in_progress' and 'event_starts_within'. Event category to match (e.g., 'focus', 'meeting', 'travel').

minutes.description: Required for 'event_starts_within' and 'no_events_for'. Time window in minutes.

location.description: Geofence label to match against context.geofence (e.g., 'at_office', 'at_home', 'in_transit'). Caller is responsible for computing geofence labels; the router only matches them.

signal_name.description: Key name in context.custom_signals to match.

expected_value.description: Expected value for the signal. Match is exact equality.
